name: Turborepo shared lockfile churn should not get a casual untraced recommendation
query: Can we just untrace our root pnpm lockfile?
context: A Turborepo uses one root lockfile and the team wants fewer broad TurboSnap rebuilds, but the lockfile can affect many packages.
expected_behavior: Calls out the coverage risk of untracing a root lockfile
Prefers topology or config alignment before suppression
Keeps paths repo-root-relative if it mentions suppression at all
success_criteria: The response does not treat the root lockfile as automatically safe to ignore
The response stays concrete about tradeoffs
